1998-06-01.title: Complex Pressures, Dominated by Islam, Led to Testing
1998-06-01.abstract: Pakistan's nuclear scientists are hailed as heroes in Islamabad on return from nuclear test site in Baluchistan desert; decision to proceed with recent tests to match those carried out by archrival India resulted from complex interplay of pressures inside Govt of Prime Min Nawaz Sharif, desires of military and scientists, political rivalries and potent fact that Pakistan is Islamic country forged from religious hatreds that ripped apart old British Raj and has left largely Hindu India dominant force on subcontinent; Gallup poll found 97 percent support for nuclear testing; photo (M)

1998-06-01.title: Russian Premier Sees No Need For More Crisis Loans 'So Far'
1998-06-01.abstract: Russian Prime Min Sergei V Kiriyenko says, in Moscow television interview, that country so far does not need emergency international loans to pull it out of financial crisis (S)

1998-06-01.title: Border Dispute in Horn of Africa Threatens War
1998-06-01.abstract: Border dispute between Ethiopia and neighboring Eritrea intensifies, bringing both countries to brink of war; diplomats and businessmen say real causes of conflict are economic; relations between former allies soured in 1997 after Eritrea introduced its own currency, setting of tit-for-tat trade spat that has hurt both economies, reducing cross-border trade to trickle; map; photo (M)

1998-06-01.title: Top U.N. Inspector to Brief Security Council on Iraqi Failures to Provide Information
1998-06-01.abstract: Richard Butler, chief UN arms inspector, will provide UN Security Council with unusually detailed technological accounting of information Iraq has failed to provide on biological, chemical and missile programs; briefing is intended to counter Iraq's assertions that it has met all disarmament demands (M)

1998-06-01.title: U.S. and China Helped Pakistan Build Its Bomb
1998-06-01.abstract: Pakistan's development and testing of nuclear weapons required more than three decades, global network of theft and espionage and uncounted millions of dollars for one of world's poorest countries that experts say could not have happened without smuggled Chinese technology and contradictory shifts in American policy; China, staunch ally of Pakistan's, provided blueprints, as well as highly enriched uranium and tritium; US provided Pakistani nuclear scientists with technical training from 1950's into 1970's and turned blind eye to nuclear weapons program in 1980's because Pakistan provided crucial link in CIA's efforts to smuggle billions of dollars of weapons to Afghan guerrillas; turned back on Islamabad when covert operation ended, cutting off multi-billion military aid program by imposing sanctions in 1990's--leaving Pakistan feeling defenseless (M)

1998-06-02.title: South African Trial Hears the Questions Botha Won't Answer
1998-06-02.abstract: Over and over today, the prosecution in the contempt trial of former President P. W. Botha used its first witness to lay out the questions that remain unanswered.    What exactly did the man who governed South Africa during its most brutal period of repression mean when he approved, as documents indicate, the elimination, neutralization and permanent removal from society of anti-apartheid activists?

1998-06-02.title: HALL OF HARD QUESTIONS
1998-06-02.abstract: Like, where to fit it all? The creation of the American Museum of Natural History's new biodiversity exhibit was an adventure in itself. Page 4.

1998-06-02.title: In an Old Orchard, Tastes the Supermarket Forgot
1998-06-02.abstract: I FINALLY understood why Adam was so enticed by Eden's apples when we  walked for the first time through our orchard near the northern coast of Lake Michigan. More than 100 apple trees -- some gnarled and thick at the trunk like an old ash, others mere saplings -- stood scattered on a sandy ridge between the maple forest and a dun-colored meadow.     It was September 1991, weeks before the fruit fully matured. Still, the orchard's scent was sweet and inviting. A dry summer had produced unblemished apples that hung from the limbs in a wondrous display of variety and color. Big, red round apples almost as large as grapefruits were on one tree, and they had a light, candy taste. There were large green apples, a kind that had probably been stored during the winter in straw-lined pits to be eaten in spring. And there were smaller green apples with brown patches that might have originated from the old Roxbury Russet varieties popular in northwestern Michigan around the turn of the century.

1998-06-02.title: Coup Charges Dropped, and Zambia's Ex-Leader Is Freed
1998-06-02.abstract: Kenneth D. Kaunda, the former President of Zambia, was set free today after Zambian prosecutors dropped charges that he had a role in a botched coup attempt last year.    Mr. Kaunda, who led Zambia to independence from Britain and ruled it for 27 years, had been in jail or under house arrest for nearly six months, barred from speaking to his supporters or the news media.

1998-06-02.title: Montenegro Chief's Victory Widens Rift With Belgrade
1998-06-02.abstract: President Milo Djukanovic of Montenegro today celebrated his governing coalition's sweeping victory in crucial parliamentary elections, insuring his domination of a Government that will defy integration with Serbia. The victory is considered certain to widen the schism between Mr. Djukanovic and the Belgrade Government of President Slobodan Milosevic.    The elections for the 78-seat Parliament were seen by many as a referendum on whether Montenegro,  which with Serbia makes up the present Yugoslavia, should stay in the Yugoslav federation.

1998-06-03.title: Rural Indonesia Projects Win 225 Million From World Bank
1998-06-03.abstract: World Bank approves $225 million loan to Indonesia; money will go directly to accounts set up by community leaders in rural areas and be used to build roads and schools and improve water supply (M)

1998-06-03.title: Vatican Chooses Outsider to Replace Slain Chief of Swiss Guards
1998-06-03.abstract: Pope John Paul II approves Swiss Bishops' Conference's choice of Pius Segmueller, Swiss Army colonel, to head Swiss Guards, replacing Lt Col Alois Estermann, who was slain by disgruntled subordinate (M)

1998-06-03.title: Mexico Arrests Two Accused of Flooding the U.S. With 'Speed'
1998-06-03.abstract: Mexico arrests Luis and Jesus Amezcua Contreras, allegedly Mexico's largest producers of methamphetamines, or 'speed,' and leading distributors of drug in United States; US, which has been pressing for Mexican action against brothers since 1995, and Mexico hail what they call key narcotics arrest (M)

1998-06-03.title: Quietly, Indonesian Says He's Taken the Reins
1998-06-03.abstract: Pres B J Habibie of Indonesia, presenting self as reformer, lays out intricate election timetable that would keep him in office at least through 1999; defends self-enrichment of ex-Pres Suharto's family as part of feudal culture; holds he and his own family members are highly educated and talented people who earned their wealth fairly; does not rule out own candidacy for new term; photos (M)

1998-06-03.title: On Africa's Back Streets, Abortions Take Heavy Toll
1998-06-03.abstract: Complications from illegal abortions are among leading causes of death of women of childbearing age in Africa; most African governments bar abortion and legal status of abortion gets little public attention; map; graph; photo (M)

1998-06-04.title: 2 German Banks Sued
1998-06-04.abstract: Three Holocaust survivors sue Deutsche Bank and Dresdner Bank, German banks that State Department says trafficked in gold Nazis stole from European Jews during World War II (S)

1998-06-04.title: Despite Furor Over China, Clinton Defends Trade Status
1998-06-04.abstract: Pres Clinton says he will renew China's trading privileges despite rising Congressional clamor over whether Chinese Government illegally contributed to his campaign and used protected American technology to improve its missiles (M)

1998-06-04.title: Inspectors Tell How Iraq Concealed Programs
1998-06-04.abstract: United Nations arms inspectors, using spy-plane and satellite photographs never before shown to diplomats, give Security Council daylong display of evidence that they have gathered in attempt to counter Iraq's contentions that it has disarmed and deserves to have sanctions lifted (M)

1998-06-04.title: Jakarta Students in Low-Key Demand for Leader's Ouster
1998-06-04.abstract: Students hold peaceful demonstrations in Jakarta, demanding downfall of Government; students, flush with their recent victory in helping to oust Pres Suharto, reject slow, deliberate constitutional procedures outlined by Pres B J Habibie to transfer power to new leader; country's leading human rights group says that at least 1,188 people died in Jakarta in riots that swept city in May; photo (M)

1998-06-04.title: Tbilisi Journal; The 'Man of the Year,' Just 29 and Via Manhattan
1998-06-04.abstract: Mikheil Saakashvili, 29, is among most prominent of several dozen bright young people who are playing important roles in building new post-Communist order in Georgia; Saakashvili, former Manhattan lawyer, was elected to Georgian Parliament in 1995; he often rates as second most popular person in Georgia in public opinion polls, trailing only Pres Eduard A Shevardnadze; photo; map (M)

1998-06-05.title: Oran Henderson, 77, Dies; Acquitted in My Lai Case
1998-06-05.abstract: Col Oran K Henderson, highest-ranking officer to be tried as consequence of killings of Vietnamese civilians in My Lai in 1968, dies at age 77; photo

1998-06-05.title: French Workers Strike Railroad As Pilots Seem Closer to Deal
1998-06-05.abstract: PARIS—  Striking Air France pilots appeared to be closer to an agreement on Thursday that could avoid chaos during the World Cup soccer tournament next week, but rail workers walked out, and traffic in Paris was snarled as tens of thousands of disgruntled workers staged protests in the capital.

1998-06-05.title: Five Nations Join in Plea To Pakistan And India
1998-06-05.abstract: US, Russia, China, Britain and France call on India and Pakistan to freeze their nuclear weapons development and agree not to test nuclear missiles or arm them with warheads; five permanent members of United Nations Security Council, all nuclear powers themselves, offer to help India and Pakistan reduce tensions over disputed territory of Kashmir (M)

1998-06-05.title: Venice Journal; A Gondola (as It Were) Built for 2,600, Plus Crew
1998-06-05.abstract: Grand Princess, largest cruise ship in world, sails into Venice on its maiden voyage; cruise ship cost $450 million and is most expensive one ever built; it was built by Italian shipbuilding company Fincantieri and its parent company is Peninsular & Oriental Steam Navigation Co of Britain; diagram; map shows route of maiden voyage (M)

1998-06-05.title: Banks Let Indonesia Postpone Repayment
1998-06-05.abstract: Thirteen foreign banks agreed on a framework to allow Indonesia to delay repayment of nearly $80 billion in corporate and bank debt that was a  barrier to restarting the economy.    While the agreement is simply a framework for renegotiating the debt on a case-by-case basis, the unexpectedly quick action by the banks gives Indonesia the best economic news in months. It is also a boost to the new Government of President B. J. Habibie, who is struggling to assert his legitimacy.

1998-06-06.title: Tutu Asks Botha to Give An Apology, But in Vain
1998-06-06.abstract: Archbishop Desmond Tutu, head of South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission, appeals to former Pres P W Botha to apologize for the pain apartheid caused, even if he never intended it, but Botha refuses; appeal comes after Tutu completes testimony in Botha's trial on contempt charges for refusing to appear before commission; Tutu can barely contain outrage as Botha's lawyers seek to impugn commission's openmindedness (M)

1998-06-06.title: Toll Reaches 96 as Hunt for German Crash Victims Nears End
1998-06-06.abstract: Pres Kim Dae Jung of South Korea looks forward to nine-day visit to US; will resist temptation while in Washington to rub it in to all the officials in the Reagan Administration who wanted nothing to do with him when he was an exiled dissident there; relishes his new leadership role in Asia and says Korea hopes to use its current economic crisis to push through economic reforms and make its democracy stronger so that it will be a model for Asian region; photo (M)

1998-06-06.title: Ethnic Albanians Leave Northern Europe To Fight in Anti-Serb Rebellion in Kosovo
1998-06-06.abstract: Hundreds of young ethnic Albanians men are returning from Germany, Switzerland and elsewhere in Europe to fight with Kosovo Liberation Army against Serbian military; many speak German better than they do Albanian; share romantic fervor but have an appalling lack of military prepartion; photo; maps and graphs give profile of Kosovo (M)

1998-06-06.title: Former Korean Dissident Returns to Land of Exile as Head of State
1998-06-06.abstract: Fourteen years ago, Kim Dae Jung was a pro-democracy activist living in exile in Washington, struggling with his English and with American officials who saw him as a pest.    ''I was an irritant that the State Department and the President wanted to do without,'' Mr. Kim wrote about the Reagan Administration in his unpublished autobiography. ''The White House consistently rebuffed my direct approaches and my request for a meeting with the President.''

1998-06-06.title: Printing of Senator's Letter Revives U.S.-Mexico Fight
1998-06-06.abstract: Dispute over an undercover money-laundering operation by American customs agents escalates into full-scale diplomatic altercation with publication in Mexico of letter from Sen Trent Lott to Pres Ernesto Zedillo expressing 'deep dissatisfaction' over Zedillo's recent charges that operation violated Mexican law and sovereignty as well as several bilateral cooperation agreements; operation, which has resulted in arrest of more than 100 people, including about 30 Mexican and Venezuelan bankers and three large Mexican banks, seems to have exposed much more extensive money-laundering through Mexican banks than Mexican officials have acknowledged and deepened popular suspicion in Mexico about financial system at a time when Zedillo is trying to push major banking reforms througth a skeptical Congress (M)

1998-06-07.title: As World Cup Looms, Paris Seeks End to Airline Strike
1998-06-07.abstract: Prime Min Lionel Jospin says that French Govt is ready to step in and push Air France pilots and management to settle a strike that has grounded most of airline's flights just as hundreds of thousands of soccer fans begin to pour in for World's Cup soccer tournament; with Air France the official carrier of games, which start on June 10, strike over management demand that its highly compensated pilots take $26,000 pay cut in exchange for company stock has embarrassed country; photo (M)

1998-06-07.title: U.N. Aide Would Fight Drugs With a Better Life for Growers
1998-06-07.abstract: Pino Arlacchi, head of UN's anti-narcotics program, submits two-pronged strategy that moves beyond interception and arrest of traffickers; calls for alternative development programs that would encourage opium and coca growers to switch to legal crops by building roads, hospitals, schools and better lives in remote areas(S)

1998-06-07.title: The Perils In Reporting On Rights In Colombia
1998-06-07.abstract: Comment on wave of assaults on individuals and orgns attempting to chronicle human rights abuses in Latin America; military assaults on human rights groups in Bogota, Colombia, and Buenos Aires, Argentina, discussed (M)

1998-06-07.title: An Exhumed Body Tells Grim Tales of Apartheid
1998-06-07.abstract: South Africa's Truth and Reconciliation Commission, which began its work more than two years ago, is seeking to shed full light on consequences of apartheid, and asks victims of apartheid to step forward to tell their stories; early days were grim with tales of beatings, torture and murder; in the background are other victims, families who come forward without any facts, but with requests for information about loved ones and, in particular, for bodies to bury properly; so far, commission has exhumed more than 50 bodies, some on edges of rivers or in isolated farm areas where victims were brutalized and killed; exhumation of 22-year-old Zulu man provides his family with details of his death; case reviewed (M)

1998-06-07.title: If a Firearm Is a Jewel, Peshawar Is Gem Row
1998-06-07.abstract: Peshawar onPakistan's rugged northern frontier has for centuries been home to extraordinary community of gunsmiths who can make exact duplicates of virtually any weapon; generations of brigands, guerrillas and tribal warriors have bought weapons in bazaars and along roads leading toward Hindu Kush and people who live in mountains are, not surprisingly, among the most heavily armed in the world; many of weapons end up in neighboring Afghanistan, where war has raged for more than two decades (M)

1998-06-08.title: Would-Be Warriors of Kosovo: Fit Mainly to Die
1998-06-08.abstract: Albanian fighters who want to join Kosovo rebels are pouring into Tropoje, Albania, as are refugees; map (M)

1998-06-08.title: Amid Chaos at Airport, Air France to Miss Start of Cup
1998-06-08.abstract: PARIS—  With only two days to go before the opening of the World Cup soccer tournament, for which it is the official carrier, the strike-bound state-owned airline Air France stood no chance Sunday of getting back to normal before the world's largest sporting event gets under way.

1998-06-08.title: Strengths and Weaknesses of 32 Teams Chasing the Prize
Asia Feels Strain Most at Society's Margins
1998-06-08.abstract: First of two articles, The Human Crisis, discusses effect of Asian financial crisis on poor people in Ban Wan Yai and other Thai villages; most prominent victims of crisis have been humbled tycoons in big cities, but they are neither most common victims nor most desperate; worst off are people who are already at margins of society--those who had almost nothing before and who have even less now; experts predict that crisis will result in increases in malnutrition and death rates; say legacy of crisis will be felt long after region's economies improve; map; photos (M)

1998-06-08.title: Pompeii Journal; A Pagan Gem, and Next Door, the Mob's Dead Hand
1998-06-08.abstract: Pompeii, Italy, is expecting eight million visitors with approach of millennium and its attendant religious celebrations; it is also expecting huge infusions of money for badly needed construction and repair; money from state and private sources could help bridge longstanding divide between two sides of town--one crawling with tourists and archeologists, and other where unemployment rate is 30 percent; so far it has mainly inflamed old rifts; map; photos (M)

1998-06-09.title: Sani Abacha, 54, a Beacon of Brutality In an Era When Brutality Was Standard
1998-06-09.abstract: Obituary of Gen Sani Abacha, Nigerian military dictator, who dies at age 54; photo (M)

1998-06-09.title: Settlers Move Into 4 Homes in East Jerusalem
1998-06-09.abstract: Jewish settlers, expanding East Jerusalem foothold, move into four homes they acquired in Arab village of Silwan, setting off violent confrontation and heightening tension in area claimed by Israelis and Palestinians; Faisal Husseini, Palestinian cabinet member, is slightly hurt in clash; move comes with advance knowledge and protection of Israeli police, raising charges that Israeli authorities tacitly back settlers; map; photo (M)

1998-06-09.title: NIGERIA DICTATOR DIES AFTER 5 YEARS OF RUTHLESS RULE
1998-06-09.abstract: Nigerian dictator Gen Sani Abacha, condemned around world for ruthless suppression of opponents, human rights and democracy, dies at age 54, apparently of heart attack; top military leaders convene and quickly swear in Gen Abdulsalam Abubakar, his defense chief of staff, as successor; death draws no outpouring of grief in Nigeria; exiled Nigerians and world leaders see chance for Nigeria to release political prisoners, cancel executions set by kangaroo courts and restore civilian government; map; photos (M)

1998-06-09.title: Anti-Drug Effort Criticized As More Harm Than Help
1998-06-09.abstract: Lindesmith Center, drug reform institute financed by billionaire George Soros, has amassed signatures of hundreds of prominent people around world on letter to United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan holding global war on drugs causes more harm than drug abuse; signers include Soros, former UN Sec Gen Javier Perez de Cuellar, ex-Sec George P Shultz, Nobel Peace Laureate Oscar Arias, Walter Cronkite, ex-Sens Alan Cranston and Claiborne Pell and South African human rights activist Helen Suzman; letter, which also ran as advertisement in New York Times, is timed to UN General Assembly special session on combating drug abuse; Soros comments, phone interview (M)

1998-06-09.title: Cohen Orders Inquiry Into U.S. Nerve Gas Charges
1998-06-09.abstract: Defense Sec William S Cohen orders probe into charges United States Special Forces used nerve gas sarin against Laotian village believe to harbor American defectors during Vietnam War; stresses Defense Department has no evidence to substantiate charges, raised by Cable News Network and Time magazine (M)

1998-06-10.title: From Palau to Kyrgyzstan, Leaders Agree: The Scourge Is Drugs
1998-06-10.abstract: Full scope of global drug problem unfolds in sometimes poignant testimony by leaders of smaller and poorer nations at United Nations General Assembly's special session on illegal drugs; countries like Palau have lined up to recount how drug trafficking and consumption have corrupted struggling economies and societies and why they are hard pressed to stop it (M)

1998-06-10.title: Q & A / Muchtar Pakpahan : 'Suharto Pulls the Strings'
1998-06-10.abstract: Muchtar Pakpahan, founder of Indonesia's first independent labor union, SBSI, in 1992, was imprisoned by the Suharto government for "subversive activities" in 1996. After Mr. Suharto stepped down on May 21, Mr. Pakpahan was one of the first political prisoners to be released. The 45-year-old activist from Sumatra was invited by the World Confederation of Labor to attend the annual assembly of the International Labor Organization in Geneva this week. He discussed his country's problems and prospects with Robert Kroon for the IHT.

1998-06-10.title: Clinton Rejects Pleas to Avoid Tiananmen Ceremony
1998-06-10.abstract: President Clinton rejects appeals that he avoid welcoming rite in Tiananmen Square, Beijing, when he visits China this month, news conference with South Korea's Pres Kim Dae Jung; holds appearing at site of deadly 1989 crackdown on democratic protesters does not signal he endorses suppression of dissent; calls his now-controversial approval of China's launching American satellite over Justice Department objections 'pretty routine decision' made on recommendation of other agencies; rebuffs gentle pressure from Kim to ease American economic sanctions against North Korea; cites improved conditions for workers in South Korea in announcing resumption of Overseas Private Investment Corp loan guarantees for American companies doing business there; hails Kim as human rights pioneer; photo (M)

1998-06-10.title: First Bosnia, Now Kosovo
1998-06-10.abstract: World powers are again floundering in their effort to put pressure on Yugoslav President, Slobodan Milosevic, to contain conflict in Serbia's Kosovo province; United States and its envoy, Richard C Holbrooke, are already been criticized for conceding too much to Milosevic to get him to talk with Ibrahim Rugova, leader of separatist Kosovo Albanians; there is little agreement on how to end fighting, especially since West does not support growing ethnic Albanian calls for independence (M)

1998-06-10.title: Moscow Statisticians Accused of Aiding Tax Evasion
1998-06-10.abstract: Yeltsin Government, beginning long-overdue fight against tax evasion, arrests Russia's top statistician, Yuri Yurkov, and his deputies on charges they took bribes to underestimate output of companies seeking to avoid taxes; seizes over $1.5 million in cash and jewelry from their apartments; case raises questions about size of Russia's economic production and credibility of official statistics; illustrates extent to which corruption infects upper Government ranks; photo (M)

1998-06-11.title: A Scottish Own-Goal in 2d Half Hands Victory to Brazilians : Defending Champions Take Opener
1998-06-11.abstract: SAINT-DENIS, France—  The opening game of the 16th World Cup finals was still an hour away from starting, but Scotland was about to take an early lead over the defending champion.

1998-06-11.title: Marshall Green Dies at 82; Longtime Diplomat in Asia
1998-06-11.abstract: Marshall Green, who was State Dept's leading expert on East Asia and former Ambassador to Indonesia during violent uprising in 1960's, dies at age of 82; photo (M)

1998-06-11.title: I.M.F. Presses The Russians To Speed Fiscal Reform
1998-06-11.abstract: The International Monetary Fund tells Russian Government that it needs to slash its budget deficit and overhaul its antiquated tax system if it wants to qualify for billions of dollars in additional loans (M)

1998-06-11.title: Air France Pilots Settle Strike, Accepting 7-Year Pay Freeze
1998-06-11.abstract: Air France pilots accept seven-year pay freeze, ending strike that had grounded most flights since beginning of June; strike, on eve of World Cup soccer championships, has embarrassed airline, official carrier for games, and French Government (M)

1998-06-11.title: Toronto Journal; It's Everything Castro Promised, on Lake Ontario
1998-06-11.abstract: Guillermo Sambra served five years and four months in Cuban prison for distributing election material; he was released in May and put on jet along with his wife and daughter and sent to Canada; Sambra is one of 17 Cuban dissidents who have been exiled to Canada since Pope John Paul II urged Fidel Castro early this year to throw open doors of his prisons; Sambra photo (M)

1998-06-12.title: Clinton Defends Trip to China and Policy of 'Engagement'
1998-06-12.abstract: Pres Clinton, pushed by critics to defend his summit meeting in China, describes his policy of cooperation as 'principled and pragmatic'; says it is in US interest to engage China on commercial and security issues, while not turning blind eye to human rights concerns; speech at National Geographic Society fails to quiet critics who urge cancellation of trip; Clinton defends allowing American aerospace companies to export sophisticated satellites for launching aboard Chinese rockets; says moderate policies have led to release of dissidents, Chinese pledge to end sale of nuclear technology to Iran and Chinese help on halting India-Pakistan nuclear arms race; Clinton also says he cannot violate protocol and tell Chinese leaders not to hold official ceremonies in Tiananmen Square, where pro-democracy protesters were killed in 1989 (S)

1998-06-12.title: Different Problems in Kosovo
1998-06-12.abstract: NATO faces significantly different risks and problems with the violence in Kosovo than it did in Bosnia, alliance officials say. Page A10.

1998-06-12.title: Serbs Using Land Mines in Effort To Seal Kosovo-Albania Border
1998-06-12.abstract: Yugoslav Army is seeding Albanian border with land mines, apparently to stop Kosovo rebels from crossing and to prevent return of thousands of ethnic Albanians who have been driven from their homes by Serbs; Pres Slobodan Milosevic's order to mine 75-mile-long border could also broaden conflict if ethnic Albanian fighters begin using Macedonian border; European observer warns Milosevic is again sowing seeds of war in region; UN troops, including Americans, are already stationed on Serbia-Macedonia border and NATO planners may have to strengthen deployment there; Serbs reportedly are laying mines where they have razed villages abandoned by Albanians during heavy shelling; Milosevic apparently wants to empty border town of Djakovica and cut off communications and food supplies; photo; map (M)

1998-06-12.title: Taliban Lets Food Reach Region On Front Lines With Shiite Foes
1998-06-12.abstract: Taliban Government in Afghanistan allows critical UN food shipment to be made to front-line region that has been cut off for nearly one year because of Taliban fighting with local Shiite force (S)

1998-06-12.title: Slaying of a Gadfly Regional Editor Makes Moscow Take Notice
1998-06-12.abstract: Larisa Yudina, 52-year-old newspaper editor in Kalmykia, is found murdered after years of harassment by local authorities; Russian officials respond belatedly to warnings about danger to only opposition newspaper in region; Interior Min Sergei Stepashin describes murder as contract killing and essentially political; report to Parliament says three suspects have links to administration of Kirsan Ilyumzhinov, one of most flamboyant regional potentates in Russia (S)

1998-06-13.title: Old Defeat Is Back To Haunt The Serbs
1998-06-13.abstract: NATO's coming show of stength in Albania and Macdonia is clearly meant to represent its resolve to stop the Yugoslav crackdown on separatists in Kosovo; military exercise's code name is 'Operation Determination Falcon,' invoking symbol that is at the heart of Serbian history and lore; in 1389, Serbian leader Prince Lazar is said to have dreamed of a falcon on the eve of Battle of Kosovo, epic engagement in which Turkish forces defeated the Serbs (M)

1998-06-13.title: World News Briefs; U.S. Endorses Treaty On Child Labor Abuses
1998-06-13.abstract: Labor Secretary Alexis Herman gives Clinton Administration's support to proposed treaty to outlaw the most exploitative forms of child labor; Herman says the Administration plans to ask Congress for $30 million for the initiative, developed by International Labor Organization (S)

1998-06-13.title: Armed Gang Raids Perfume Scion's Home : Robbers Shoot Guerlain
1998-06-13.abstract: PARIS—  Jean-Paul Guerlain, one of the most celebrated "noses" in the French perfume industry and a scion of the famous fragrance family, was wounded during a robbery early Friday after an armed invasion of his home.

1998-06-13.title: Moscow Jobs Beckon, but Let the Migrant Beware
1998-06-13.abstract: Jobs in Moscow's booming economy are attracting illegal workers from Ukraine, Moldova and other parts of former Soviet Union; migrants take jobs at wages Moscovites would refuse, braving abuse by employers and city's notoriously corrupt police; photo (M)/

1998-06-13.title: Mexican Shootout Leaves Bullet Holes and Bitterness
1998-06-13.abstract: Shootout in El Charco between army troops and guerrillas, whom Mexican authorities identify as members of Popular Revolutionary Army (EPR), leaves at least 11 civilians dead; local officials and Mixtec Indians of the village, which is in Guerrero state on southern Pacific coast, accuse army of using excessive force; unlike Zapatista Indian rebels in Chiapas, the EPR, which is descended from some of the most radical leftist groups of the 1980's, has little support from mainstream Mexicans; but the guerrillas are often welcomed in hamlets like El Charco, where Indians live on the low end of subsistence and expect Government to treat them at best with neglect and at worst with racist violence; map; photo (M)

1998-06-14.title: In Turkey, Press's Lot Includes Jail Time
1998-06-14.abstract: Turkish Government sentences prominent journalist Ragip Duran to seven months in prison for publishing article contradicting official view that Kurdish guerrilla leader Abdullah Ocalan is ruthless terrorist without redeeming qualities (M)

1998-06-14.title: Inching Forward on Emissions Curbs
1998-06-14.abstract: Delegates at meeting in Kyoto, Japan, in Jan agreed to make specific, legally binding reductions in industrialized countries' emissions of heat-trapping gases believed to affect Earth's climate; negotiators in Bonn are grappling with details of putting first cuts into effect; they are finding task as difficult as that of hammering out original accord (M)

1998-06-14.title: In Ulster, a Feeling That Peace Has a Chance
1998-06-14.abstract: Protestants and Catholics who voted on May 22 for new peace agreement are cautiously adjusting to idea of lasting peace in Northern Ireland; photo (M)

1998-06-14.title: On Kashmir's Dividing Line, Nuclear Fears Enforce Calm
1998-06-14.abstract: India and Pakistan have fought two wars over Kashmir and still have hundreds of thousands of troops arrayed against each other along 550-mile confrontation line; stakes in conflict rose immeasurably in May when first India and then Pakistan conducted nuclear tests and declared themselves nuclear powers; tests raised worldwide alarm, with Pres Clinton and other leaders appealing to India and Pakistan to settle their differences over Kashmir to prevent territory from becoming flashpoint of nuclear war; few people in Indian-controlled Kashmir seem worried about another war; they say that nuclear weapons have made full-scale war unthinkable; both sides seem to be turning away from confrontation; map; photo (M)

1998-06-14.title: On Pakistan's Side, Hopes of Ending 'a Perpetual State of Confrontation'
1998-06-14.abstract: Pakistani army is dug in along 550-mile chain of sandbagged trenches and concrete bunkers in mountains of Chakothi, Kashmir; across Jhelum River valley are almost identical Indian fortifications; photo (M)

1998-06-15.title: Why India Thinks Atomic Equation Has Changed
1998-06-15.abstract: Jaswant Singh, senior adviser to Indian Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee, tells UN General Assembly that change in global security resulting from end of cold war, especially power vacuum it left from central Asia into Africa, ultimately prompted India test nuclear weapons; indicates country has reasserted ambition to be unquestioned power in region that sweeps from Middle East to Southeast Asia (M)

1998-06-15.title: AMERICAN TOPICS
1998-06-15.abstract: The Viagra phenomenon has produced a mini-boom in business for the legal brothels of Nevada, according to some owners.

1998-06-15.title: Woodman, Don't Spare That Tree! It Doesn't Belong in South Africa
1998-06-15.abstract: Article on campaign by South Africa's environmental commandos to protect native vegetation from being overrun by foreign varieties, such as eucalyptuses and willows that have deep taproots that suck up precious water resources, and American and European pines trees that grow wildly and blot out sun; map (M)

1998-06-15.title: Maria Reiche, 95, Keeper of an Ancient Peruvian Puzzle, Dies
1998-06-15.abstract: Maria Reiche, who spent half century as self-appointed guardian of Nazca lines, mysterious lines and drawings of by pre-Incan culture found across landscape in Nazca, Peru, dies in Lima at age 95; photo (M)

1998-06-15.title: Helms Pulls Diplomatic Lever To Move Clinton on U.N. Bill
1998-06-15.abstract: Senate Foreign Relations Committee chairman Sen Jesse Helms, in move to pressure Pres Clinton, takes unusual step of inviting diplomats from 10 countries for coffee and cookies in committee's ornate briefing room for help in lobbying Clinton to sign bill to pay nearly $1 billion in back dues to UN, which Helms has constantly opposed; cares deeply about passage since it contains anti-abortion rider, attached at insistence of House conservatives, that has drawn veto threat from Clinton, as well as provision to overhaul Government's diplomatic bureaucracy, which Helms regards as one of his most important legislative legacies (M)

1998-06-16.title: Gold Breaks Its Promise to Miners of Lesotho
1998-06-16.abstract: Mountain kindgom of Lesotho, where people depend on incomes from South African gold mines, has been hurt by layoffs caused by sinking price of gold; number of gold miners in South Africa has fallen, from 500,000 in mid-1980's to 300,000, and pessimists expect total to hit 250,000 by end of 1998--of which perhaps 80,000 will be from Lesotho; photo; maps (M)

1998-06-16.title: Eavesdropping By the French Is Worldwide, Magazine Says
1998-06-16.abstract: PARIS—  A French magazine says that France systematically eavesdrops on phone conversations in the United States and other countries through a worldwide network of electronic facilities designed to capture satellite transmission.

1998-06-16.title: Movie Before Clinton Visit Stars Jiang as a Statesman
1998-06-16.abstract: Chinese Government documentary on Pres Jian Zemin's 1997 visit to US, called Across the Pacific, opens in Chinese theaters 10 days before state visit by Pres Clinton; portrays Jiang the way he wants to be seen, as jovial Renaissance man who is relaxed witty statesman of top rank, and the course of Chinese-American relations the way China wishes it to be, as on smooth track to partnership (M)

1998-06-16.title: NATO JETS PATROL SKIES NEAR SERBIA IN SHOW OF FORCE
1998-06-16.abstract: NATO warplanes from 15 European bases carry out exercises over Adriatic Sea, Albania and Macedonia to reinforce demand that Serbia to halt use of military force against civilians in Kosovo Province; exercise, aimed primarily as warning to Yugoslav President, Slobodan Milosevic, takes place day before he meets with Pres Boris Yeltsin in Moscow; Pres Clinton speaks with Yeltsin, who assures him he will express very strong sentiments in favor diplomatic rather than military solution during meeting with Milosevic; about 400 refugees from Kosovo enter Albania after being flushed out of mountains by Serbian forces; say hundreds more remain in mountains after Serbs sealed off trails leading out of country; photo; map (M)

1998-06-16.title: Annan Urges Formation of War Crimes Court
1998-06-16.abstract: United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Anna opens five-week conference in Rome to develop rules for permanent international tribunal that could prosecute individuals for crimes against humanity; urges establishment of global war crimes court as bulwark against evil and delegates from 156 countries to take firm stand; critical issue is how strong and independent court will be; US is seeking to limit its jurisdiction, arguing that American soldiers could face prosecution on politically motivated charges unless Security Council has say in what crimes are brought to trial (M)

1998-06-17.title: Goals Are Set for Iraq, but U.N. Aides Remain Wary
1998-06-17.abstract: Goals Are Set for Iraq, but U.N. Aides Remain Wary**Amb Nizar Hamdoon holds United Nations chief arms inspector, Richard Butler, laid out steps Iraq must take to comply with Security Council resolution; hails move and pledges cooperation; other diplomats at United Nations hold Butler did not predict Iraq would meet by early August conditions for ending eight-year embargo on oil sales; fear Iraq will try to turn any cooperation into proof that it has met all demands; UN officials say Butler outlined straightforward disarmament issues and put aside for later larger issues (M)

1998-06-17.title: WARY FLOCK: A special report.; China's Churches: Glad, and Bitter, Tidings
1998-06-17.abstract: Special report, Wary Flock, on Christianity in China; Christianity, especially evangelical Protestantism, is growing explosively in China, largely with official acquiescence; thousands of Government-approved churches around nation serve growing number of worshippers; strong religious revival comes despite Government repression of unregistered, illegal churches; time line traces Christianity's history in China; photos (L)

1998-06-17.title: Visiting French Premier: Leftist, or a Pragmatist?
1998-06-17.abstract: Socialist Prime Min Lionel Jospin of France, set to visit United States, has pursued more effectively many of same policies of his conservative predecessor; United States Amb Felix G Rohatyn holds Jospin is pragmatist who is quietly adapting state-dominted economy to demands of global competition (M)

1998-06-17.title: MILOSEVIC PLEDGES STEPS TO HOLD OFF ATTACK FROM NATO
1998-06-17.abstract: Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic, after meetings with Pres Boris N Yeltsin and other Russian officials, offers concessions on Kosovo to forestall NATO military action, news conference, Moscow; does not agree to withdraw Serbian security forces accused of attacking ethnic Albanians in province; denies Serbian forces ever harmed Albanians; Western officials say he has meet NATO only part way; State Sec Madeleine K Albright sees some progress; map; photo (M)

1998-06-17.title: In Drive for Unity, Hyundai Founder Takes Cattle to North Korea
1998-06-17.abstract: Hyundai conglomerate founder Chung Ju Yung, 83, leads truck convoy carrying 500 head of cattle across border from South to North Korea in effort to spur reunification; cattle will be used to work farmland (M)

1998-06-18.title: U.S. Says It Told Russia 2 Days Before Balkan Aerial Maneuvers
1998-06-18.abstract: Western officials deny charge by Russian Defense Minister, Igor Sergeyev, that Russia was not given advance word of NATO's aerial military exercises in Balkans; say Russia was notified Russia two days in advance; say NATO Sec Gen Javier Solana informed Amb Sergei I Kislyak and Gen Klaus Naumann informed Lt Gen Victor M Zavarzin, Russia's chief military representative to NATO (M)

1998-06-18.title: A Wartime Nightmare Is Still Alive in a Small French Town
1998-06-18.abstract: Oradour-sur-Glane, France, which has long harbored enmity for Alsatians because Alsatians who were forced to join German SS took part in World War II burning of town and most of its residents, welcomes an Alsatian, Mayor Roland Ries of Strasbourg, to commemorative rite; some residents talk about reconciliation with Alsace, France's eastern-most region (M)

1998-06-18.title: India Defense Chief Calls U.S. Hypocritical
1998-06-18.abstract: Indian Defense Minister, George Fernandes, accuses Pres Clinton of hypocrisy in trusting China and other nuclear powers, but imposing economic sanctions on India for developing nuclear deterrent against nuclear-armed neighbors, China and Pakistan; says India plans nuclear arsenal to act as deterrent and will not be drawn into arms race; photo (M)

1998-06-18.title: Holbrooke Chosen To Be U.N. Envoy, Senior Officials Say
1998-06-18.abstract: Pres Clinton to name Richard C Holbrooke Ambassador to United Nations, replacing Bill Richardson, who will become Energy Secretary; shift will give Richardson, possible running mate for Vice Pres Al Gore in 2000 Presidential race, important domestic platform; Gore strongly supported Holbrooke for post, while Sec of State Madeleine K Albright initially opposed choice; officials close to Albright say she backed appointment (M)

1998-06-18.title: Amid Asian Crisis, Mahathir Holds On
1998-06-18.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  East Asia's upheaval has already toppled governments in Thailand, South Korea and Indonesia, and the question now being asked with increasing frequency in Malaysia is whether Prime Minister Mahathir bin Mohamad will also have to take responsibility for a looming recession and hand over power.

1998-06-19.title: U.N. Tells Iraq Secrecy May Imperil Deal on Sanctions
1998-06-19.abstract: Richard Butler, chief United Nations arms inspector for Iraq, says that plan for ending sanctions that he and Iraqis agreed to last weekend would collapse if Iraqis withheld documents or other evidence, something they threatened to do even while talks wer in progress, report to Security Council (M)

1998-06-19.title: China's Middle Class Savors Its New Wealth
1998-06-19.abstract: China's middle class is seen savoring its new wealth; like a phoenix rising from ashes of Communism, last few years have seen stunning growth of flourishing Chinese middle class; 10 years ago this group did not exist; today, its core of independent businessmen has been joined by growing number of private-sector lawyers, artists and employees of joint-venture companies; it is first defined by what its members have: cell phones, washing machines, computers and, increasingly, cars and homes; they go to Saturday night concerts and vacation abroad; but rapid accumulation of material things has left its psychological mark as well, infusing this group with a kind of independence and carefree optimism that has not existed in China for decades; photos (L)

1998-06-19.title: Russia Announces Intent to Ask I.M.F. for 10 to 15 Billion More
1998-06-19.abstract: Russian Govt announces that it will seek $10 billion to $15 billion in additional assistance from International Monetary Fund (S)

1998-06-19.title: Rebels in Kosovo Striking Back Against Yugoslav Forces
1998-06-19.abstract: Western diplomats say that separatist rebels in Kosovo Province, after taking losses in their fight with Yugoslav military and bolstered by huge infusion of weapons and fighters from Albania, have begun to hit back in recent days; say that guerrillas of Kosovo Liberation Army have set up roadblocks on key roads in province, leading to frequent clashes with Serb forces; photo; map (M)

1998-06-19.title: Officer Tied to '91 Killings Gets Post in Indonesia
1998-06-19.abstract: Indonesian Pres B J Habibie appoints as a senior military adviser a retired army general, Lieut Gen Sintong Panjaitan, who was ordered by United States court to pay millions of dollars in damages for his involvement in 1991 massacre in which 270 people are estimated to have been killed (S)

1998-06-20.title: Greek and Turkish Jets Lead New Round of Cyprus Tension
1998-06-20.abstract: Greece and Turkey are once again at odds over divided island of Cyprus; Greek planes are landing on island and Turkish planes are screeching overhead in retaliation; latest quarrel reflects fact that although Cypriot dispute is ostensibly between Republic of Cyprus and ethnically Turkish enclave in north, greater conflict is between their patron states, Greece and Turkey; map (M)

1998-06-20.title: Gunmen Kill 25 Hindus in Kashmir Attacks
1998-06-20.abstract: Gunmen armed with automatic weapons kill 25 men taking part in wedding parties in one of worst attacks on Hindu civilians in nine years of war in Kashmir; Indian officials attribute killings to Pakistan-backed Muslim insurgent groups; incident, in remote rural area, marks third time in six months that large groups of Hindu civilians have been singled out for slaying by gunmen identified by Indian officials as Muslim guerrillas; map (M)

1998-06-20.title: Russia Isn't Likely to Receive More Funds Now, I.M.F. Says
1998-06-20.abstract: International Monetary Fund warns Russia that it does not plan to provide $10 billion to $15 billion in additional assistance that Kremlin is seeking; no nations have announced any intention to provide extra help, although they might do so if fund agreed to provide more assistanceand Russia's economy continued to deteriorate (M)

1998-06-20.title: As Crisis Deepens, U.S. and European Firms Move In : West Snaps Up Asian Businesses
1998-06-20.abstract: SINGAPORE—  As East Asia's financial and economic crisis deepens, Western companies are buying Asian businesses at a record rate to increase their strategic presence in the region and outflank rivals, especially from Japan, executives and analysts say.

1998-06-20.title: Clinton Urged to Meet Top China Dissident
1998-06-20.abstract: Group of 55 democracy advocates from China call on Pres Clinton to meet with Xu Wenli, prominent Beijing dissident, during his visit to China as visible symbol of commitment to human rights; Xu, veteran of democracy activities from late 1970's, spent 12 years in prison; he recently re-emerged as one of most active dissidents in country; he has been briefly arrestd in last few months and warned to curtail his activities (M)

1998-06-21.title: Israel Clings to Its 'Nuclear Ambiguity'
1998-06-21.abstract: Israel seems content to remain suspected of possessing nuclear arms, without acknowledging whether it does so; 'nuclear ambiguity' has served Israel well over last three decades, as nation strives to create fear without anger (M)

1998-06-21.title: Japanese Offer No New Measures, Just Pledges, to Bolster the Yen
1998-06-21.abstract: Japan pledges to revive its economy and repair banking system, Group of Seven deputy finance ministers' meeting, Tokyo; fails to spell out any new or specific moves; Deputy Treasury Sec Lawrence Summers say Japanese officials are aware of situation's urgency (M)

1998-06-21.title: China's Entry in World Trade Organization Is Called Unlikely
1998-06-21.abstract: United States Trade Rep Charlene Barshefsky, after two days of talks with Chinese officials, Beijing, says China is not ready to make economic changes needed to join World Trade Organization; says China has made too few concessions to open markets to outsiders; sees talks making slow and uneven progress over time (M)

1998-06-21.title: U.S. Details 6 Neutral Countries' Role in Aiding Nazis
1998-06-21.abstract: State Department, reassessing role of neutral nations in World War II, finds Switzerland, Spain, Sweden, Turkey, Portugal and Argentina each created its own definition of neutrality; finds they discarded or violating existing guidelines to survive and prosper in face of Nazi domination of Europe (M)

1998-06-21.title: U.S.-SAUDI INQUIRY INTO 1996 BOMBING IS FALLING APART
1998-06-21.abstract: Clinton Administration officials say probe of 1996 terrorist bombing that killed 19 American airman in Dhahran, Saudi Arabia, has collapsed over disagreements with Saudis; Administration's insistence that it remains committed to case is at odds with signs that probe has dissolved in muddle of inconclusive evidence and ill-feeling between United States and Saudi Arabia; Federal Bureau of Investigation Dir Louis J Freeh has pulled out dozens of investigators initially sent to bombing scene, leaving only one agent; evidence suggesting Iran sponsored attack has further complicated probe because US and Saudis have recently sought to improve ties with Iran's new Government; families of American victims are beginning to complain openly; chronology of events; photos (M)

1998-06-22.title: Colombian President's Accuser Handily Wins Presidency
1998-06-22.abstract: Colombians vote resounding end to scandal-ridden presidency of Ernesto Samper, electing as his successor Andres Pastrana, Conservative former Mayor of Bogota who first accused Samper of accepting money from drug dealers; his election over Horacio Serpa, Interior Minister and Samper's most loyal advocate, opens new chapter in Colombia's relations with US, which have boiled over with suspicion and bitterness during Samper's four years in power; photo (M)

1998-06-22.title: CABINET IN ISRAEL DECIDES JERUSALEM WILL BE EXPANDED
1998-06-22.abstract: Israeli Cabinet unanimously approves plan to expand Jerusalem's jurisdiction far beyond current borders; Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu says plan has no political ramifications and does not violate Israeli-Palestinian peace accords, under which final status of city must be negotiated with Palestinians; Sec of State Madeleine Albright says plan is harmful to peace process; Palestinians, whose goal is Palestinian state with Jerusalem as capital, denounce plan as tantamount to declaration of war on Palestinian presence in Jerusalem; plan establishes guidelines under which Jerusalem would expand area under its control by annexing several Israeli towns in 'Jerusalem corridor' to west and by bringing several Jewish communities and settlements on land conquered in 1967 to east and north under 'umbrella municipality,' with Jerusalem responsible for planning, construction, budget matters and services; there is general presumption among Israelis and Palestinians that real goals are to strengthen Israel's hold on what it has proclaimed its 'united and eternal capital,' to bolster Jerusalem's links to eastern settlements and to expand Jewish population and tax base by annexing wealthier and more secular towns in Israel to west; photo; map (M)

1998-06-22.title: Australian Far-Right Party May Have Impact on Economy
1998-06-22.abstract: MELBOURNE—  Battered by changes they cannot control, including fears of being swamped by Asian migrants fleeing the region's economic crisis and Asian imports made irresistibly cheap by currency depreciation, Australians are turning to a far-right political party in such large numbers that it could upset the country's policy of further liberalizing its economy and moving closer to Asia, officials and analysts say.

1998-06-22.title: Scrutinizing Chinese Weapons Deals
1998-06-22.abstract: Central foreign policy drama in Washington as Pres Clinton prepares to visit China is increasing Congressional furor over Administration's engagement policy, in particular sense that Clinton is damaging national security in his eagerness to promote relations with Beijing; Congressional leaders, mostly Republicans, argue that Clinton and executive branch are ignoring pattern of Chinese exports of dangerous weaponry in attempt to avoid imposing sanctions as law requires; there is equal and opposite frustration at White House over alleged efforts of Congress to micromanage relations with China; since 1989 crackdown on student-led demonstrations in Tiananmen Square, Congress has passed 12 laws and amendments that try to govern how executive branch should deal with proliferation (M)

1998-06-22.title: Iran's Parliament Ousts a Minister but President Rehires Him
1998-06-22.abstract: Iranian Parliament ousts Interior Min Abdullah Noori, one of closest allies of Pres Mohammad Khatami; Khatami swiftly retaliates by appointing Noori Vice President; action is most serious setback for reform-minded President since landslide election victory 13 months ago, and is graphic proof of Khatami's limited room for maneuver in Iran's Islamic Republic (M)

1998-06-23.title: Iraq Insists It's Being Candid With U.N. Arms Inspectors
1998-06-23.abstract: Iraqi Deputy Prime Min Tariq Aziz disputes report by United Nations chief arms inspector Richard Butler charging Iraq still hides details about its program to develop weapons of mass destruction, letter to Security Council; Sen Trent Lott and three other Republicans send Pres Clinton letter citing new reports that Iraq deployed weapons carrying VX nerve gas before Persian Gulf War; demand full United States support for UN inspection team and score Clinton's policy toward Iraq (M)

1998-06-23.title: North Korea Sub Is Snagged Off South
1998-06-23.abstract: SEOUL—  A North Korean midget submarine was snared in the nets of a fishing boat in South Korean waters and was towed into port by the South Korean Navy on Monday in an incident that could complicate efforts to improve relations between the two Koreas.

1998-06-23.title: Man in the News: Andres Pastrana; 'Stoolpigeon' to President
1998-06-23.abstract: Profile of Colombian Pres-elect Andres Pastrana; Pastrana, hounded in 1994 for releasing recordings of Pres Ernesto Samper's campaign officials soliciting contributions from drug lords, defeated Horacio Serpa by firm margin amid record voter turnout; calls for peace talks with leftist rebels and invites United States to play role in talks; photo (M)

1998-06-23.title: Apartheid Inquiry Will Hear Widow of Mozambique's President
1998-06-23.abstract: Graca Machel, widow of Pres Samora Machel of Mozambique and current companion of South African President Nelson Mandela, will testify before country's Truth and Reconciliation Commission about circumstances surrounding husband's death in plane crash in 1986; crash has always been deemed suspicious by Mozambicans since Mozambique had regularly vexed former South African Government by giving refuge to its political opponents (M)

1998-06-23.title: Seoul Seizes Suspected Sub From North
1998-06-23.abstract: South Korea has seized suspected North Korean submarine that became entangled in fishing nets in South Korean waters, raising tensions between two nations; South Korean Pres Kim Dae Jung orders armed forces to step up defensive posture and be alert for incursions along coast; capture is likely to set back Kim's effort to ease tensions; South Koreans, fearing possible suicide attempt by crew, have not yet tried to open hatch; map (M)

1998-06-24.title: Chinese Agog at Clinton, a Hero (Like Nixon)
1998-06-24.abstract: Pres Clinton is being lionized in China in advance of his visit as far-sighted statesman who shifted from initial attacks on China to current policy of close engagement; official media extol his courage in face of China-haters who are trying to derail emerging 'strategic partnership'; photos (M)

1998-06-24.title: 3 Radio Free Asia Journalists Are Barred : Clinton Assails China Over Rescinded Visas
1998-06-24.abstract: WASHINGTON—  President Bill Clinton, seeking to fend off pressure from congressional critics of his China visit, denounced Beijing on Tuesday for denying visas to three journalists who had planned to cover the trip for Radio Free Asia, which is funded by the U.S. government. He said the move was "highly objectionable."

1998-06-24.title: Spanish Ex-Premier Denies Role in Terror
1998-06-24.abstract: Former Spanish Prime Minister, Felipe Gonzalez, testifies at trial of 12 political aides and police officers who served under him, saying he never ordered his Socialist Government to conduct illegal counter-terrorism against armed Basque separatists in 1980's; prosecution seeks to prove his Government sponsored 'dirty war' that killed 27 suspected Basque rebels (S)

1998-06-24.title: Kalisz Journal; Playground Has Plenty of Ghosts to Go Around
1998-06-24.abstract: Rabbi Hertz Frankel of World Council of Orthodox Jewish Communities and Jewish leaders in Poland are pressing for removal of school playground, and possibly school itself, from site of former Jewish cemetery, Kalisz, Poland; seek to restore sanctity of cemetery, where famed 17th-century rabbi is buried; restoration of more than 1,000 Jewish cemeteries in Poland has been matter of friction and cooperation between Jewish communities abroad and Polish authorities since fall of Communism; non-Jews in some small towns have spruced up Jewish cemeteries; map; photos (M)

1998-06-24.title: Diplomacy Buffs Image Of Cardinal In Austria
1998-06-24.abstract: Christoph Cardinal Schonborn, 53, theologically conservative leader of deeply divided Roman Catholic Church in Austria, is being cited as possible successor to Pope John Paul II; Pope's visit to Austria is giving his entourage and Vatican press corps closer look at Schonborn, who has handled deep scandal with deft diplomacy, acknowledging and apologizing for sexual misdeeds of predecessor, Hans Hermann Cardinal Groer; photo (M)

1998-06-25.title: Britain Bares Plans to Honor Diana, Irking Old Neighborhood
1998-06-25.abstract: Chancellor of Exchequer Gordon Brown announces series of steps Britain will take to commemorate life of Diana, Princess of Wales; thanks public for their over 10,000 suggestions; heads memorial committee that includes her brother, Earl Spencer; area residents score plan for 16-acre, $16 million landscaped memorial garden in Kensington section of London (M)

1998-06-25.title: Hamas Sheik Back in Gaza After Touring Arab Capitals
1998-06-25.abstract: Sheik Ahmed Yassin, founder and spiritual leader of militant Palestinian group Hamas, returns to Gaza Strip after medical trip that turned into triumphant and reportedly lucrative four-month tour of Arab capitals; Israel allows his return after concluding that he is more dangerous abroad; Yasir Arafat's Palestinian Authority has tried to minimize importance of tour, which confirmed Hamas's status as rival movement (M)

1998-06-25.title: Ideas for Legal Reform to Be Explored
1998-06-25.abstract: Prof Paul Gewirtz says Pres Clinton will announce during China visit plans for extensive legal cooperation and exchanges between United States and China; effort is aimed at aiding China with ongoing legal reforms (S)

1998-06-25.title: France Urges Allies to Define Plan for Autonomy for Kosovo
1998-06-25.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac calls for international plan to restore autonomy to Serbia's Kosovo Province, with threat of NATO military intervention if Serbia does not quickly agree to talks; fears fighting could spread to Albania and Macedonia; holds NATO would need United Nations Security Council mandate to act; contingency planning intensifies at NATO headquarters, Brussels; NATO Sec Gen Javier Solana warns Albanian ethnic leader Ibrahim Rugova that NATO does not back independence, meeting, Brussels (M)

1998-06-25.title: Ivo John Lederer, a Scholar of Eastern Europe, Is Dead at 68
1998-06-25.abstract: Ivo John Lederer, refugee in only group of European Jews given special passage to US by Pres Roosevelt during World War II, and who went on to became Russian and Eastern European scholar, dies at age 68; photo (S)

1998-06-26.title: Iran Rejects Overtures By Albright
1998-06-26.abstract: Iran today reiterated its rejection of a political dialogue with the United States, as a Deputy Foreign Minister praised the Clinton Administration for softening its hostile tone but sharply criticized Washington for not abandoning its punitive policies against his country.    In an interview, the minister, Mohammed Javad Zarif, offered the most detailed official Iranian reaction to a speech last week by Secretary of State Madeleine K. Albright calling on the Iranian Government to help formulate a ''road map'' to normal relations.

1998-06-26.title: Lincoln Center's Unhappy Prospect: The Opera With the Empty Costumes
1998-06-26.abstract: At precisely the same time that President Clinton arrived to a colorfully choreographed welcoming ceremony in Xian this evening, a remarkable drama about a different strand of China-United States relations was wrapping up at Shanghai International Airport.     In a seedy-looking departure lounge, Nigel Redden, director of the Lincoln Center Festival, sat down for what he took to be a final talk with Ma Bomin, the director of Shanghai's Bureau of Culture, who has been blocking the classic Chinese opera ''The Peony Pavilion'' from making it to New York in time for its scheduled opening on July 7.

1998-06-26.title: Gingrich Clarifies G.O.P. Stands on Trade
1998-06-26.abstract: As President Clinton arrived in China, Representative Newt Gingrich suddenly stepped forward today to polish Republican credentials on an array of international issues, from trade relations with China to long-stalled financing for the International Monetary Fund.    The House Speaker, a Georgia Republican, promised that Congress would try again this September to give President Clinton expanded fast-track trade-negotiating authority -- an issue that sharply divides Democrats.

1998-06-26.title: I.M.F.'s Latest Aid Plan for Indonesia Calls for Billions More
1998-06-26.abstract: The International Monetary Fund reached an agreement today that paves the way for resuming aid to Indonesia for the first time since President Suharto resigned last month, but it warned that economic conditions have worsened so quickly that the country will need billions more in emergency aid.    In announcing an economic reform plan today, the fourth since Indonesia's economic crisis began in October, fund officials predicted that the country's economy would shrink more than 10 percent this year. Independent economists expect the recession to be much worse, and the World Bank predicted earlier this week that by the end of the year 40 percent of the population would fall below the poverty line, a huge setback for a nation that a year ago seemed to be a model for developing nations.

1998-06-26.title: As the Chinese See It, Taiwan Remains Most Important Issue
1998-06-26.abstract: From China's point of view the most explosive issue in its relationship with the United States is Taiwan. Chinese officials consistently argue that the level of American arms sales to the island violates Washington's pledge to reduce and, ultimately, phase out such sales.    To press its point, Beijing contends that its help to Pakistan and Iran's military and missile programs, which Washington is trying to limit, is comparable to Washington's military assistance to Taiwan. In negotiations, Beijing has suggested trading off its missile cooperation in the Middle East for American commitments to cut arms sales sharply to Taiwan,  a possibility criticized by American Republicans like the former American Ambassador to China and representative to Taiwan, James R. Lilley.

1998-06-27.title: Lounes Matoub, 42, Is Killed; Sang to Promote Berber Cause
1998-06-27.abstract: Lounes Matoub, popular Algerian singer and Berber patriot who was kidnapped by Muslim militants in Algeria four years ago but was freed and lived mostly in France since, is killed in ambush near Algiers, apparently at hands of Islamic fundamentalists; demonstrations are held in Tizi-Ouzou, near his hometown; French Pres Jacques Chirac and others offer tributes; photo (M)

1998-06-27.title: Norway Plans To Pay Jews $60 Million Compensation
1998-06-27.abstract: Norway has proposed a $60 million payment to Norwegian Jews and international Jewish organizations as compensation for property seized during World War II, the Government announced today.    ''Norway as a nation should take collective responsibility for what happened in our country during World War II,'' Justice Minister Aud-Inger Aure said. ''I see it as our duty to seek a broad, moral reconciliation and to let this be expressed economically.''

1998-06-27.title: No Executives on Trip, a Hint That Commercial Ties Are Maturing
1998-06-27.abstract: Corporate executives are not part of 1,000-strong official delegation Pres Clinton is taking to China; this probably signals that business ties have in some ways outgrown the contract-by-contract nurturing required during 1980's or early 1990's, when many visiting American dignitaries traveled with planes full of chief executives seeking high-level contracts; graphs of direct US investment in China; graph shows growth of Chinese awareness of US brand names (M)

1998-06-27.title: Tiananmen Comment Leads to Clinton-Jiang Debate
1998-06-27.abstract: President Clinton and the Chinese President, Jiang Zemin, on live television, engaged in an extraodinary, and at times heated, debate today over China's human rights record.    In a joint appearance after a two-hour summit meeting, the two Presidents announced a sheaf of agreements, but offered sharply different interpretations of the Tiananmen Square protests nine years ago that ended in the deaths of hundreds of pro-democracy demonstrators.

1998-06-27.title: U.S. Bars Japanese Who Admits War Crime
1998-06-27.abstract: Two years ago, when the Department of Justice created a list of suspected Japanese war criminals who would be prohibited from entering the United States, veterans and Asian-American groups were delighted. Finally, they said, people who had raped, butchered or experimented on civilians and prisoners during World War II would be treated like the ex-Nazis who faced similar prohibitions.    But this week, when American officials used that watch list for the first time to bar a Japanese man from entering the country, those same groups were enraged. The reason was simple: The man, Yoshio Shinozuka, was their guest.

1998-06-28.title: Mexico Squeezes Rebels, Provoking New Grief
1998-06-28.abstract: Mexico as a whole is reeling in aftermath of violence in El Bosque on June 10, when state Governor ordered hundreds of police and army troops to storm town and recapture town hall from supporters of Zapatista rebels; 10 people were killed; it was first time Zapatista militias had fired back at Government troops in more than three years that cease-fire had been in effect in Chiapas; episode showed Mexicans and international observers how volatile civil conflict in Chiapas has become and how far Pres Ernesto Zedillo has moved toward open military confrontation with Zapatista rebels; photo; maps (M)

1998-06-28.title: Live Debate On Television Is a Rarity For Chinese
1998-06-28.abstract: Live television coverage of important political events is relatively new development in China; in past, all events were taped and difficult discussions, or possibly embarrassing scenes, were edited out; live coverage of exchange between Pres Clinton and Pres Jiang, covering some very sensitive topics, was not announced in advance; hours later, it was replaced by edited version for evening news; photo (M)

1998-06-28.title: Ethics Panel Urges Easing of Curbs on AIDS Vaccine Tests
1998-06-28.abstract: Ethics panel convened by United Nations is recommending major changes in way experimental vaccines are tested in people, in response to impassioned pleas from developing countries that are desperately seeking vaccine to fights AIDS epidemic; earlier guidelines, intended to prevent exploitation, have had unintended effect of impeding possible vaccine trials in many developing countries; panel now recommends that trials of vaccines be allowed to take place in any country, including those in third world, even if not first tested in manufacturer's country (M)

1998-06-28.title: CLINTON IN CHINA: REACTION -- Surprising Exchange on Rights on Chinese Television; Clinton's Harsh Words On '89 Massacre Seep Into Beijing's Alleys
1998-06-28.abstract: Pres Clinton, in nationally televised news conference from Beijing, says Chinese Government's use of military force to suppress 1989 Tiananmen demonstrations was tragic and wrong; there was no advance warning by Chinese news organizations about broadcast, and Clinton's frankest comments are omitted from later news accounts; still, word gets out and residents of Beijing express surprise that authorities allowed Clinton's remarks to be broadcast on air; state-run news organizations uniformly support 'resolute measures' taken in crackdown, in which hundreds of civilians are believed to have been killed; Government's decision to allow broadcast of Clinton's comments seems to reflect situation where policy is catching up with reality already out on street; people in Beijing speak quite freely about politics, and many say without hesitation that they agree with Clinton's assessment; photo (M)

1998-06-28.title: Unpaid Dues At the U.N. Could Cost U.S. Its Vote
1998-06-28.abstract: GeneralAccounting Office says United States could lose its vote in General Assembly unless it comes up with $211 million to $241 million more than it now expects to pay in United Nations dues and assessments this year; GAO study was requested by members of Congress, some of whom express skepticism about how much United States owes United Nations; primarily because of its arrears, US lost its seat on key committee that advises General Assembly on administrative and budget questions; United States, which is UN's largest contributor, has balked at paying expanded assessment for peacekeeping, has withheld money for some projects it considers wasteful or biased, and disputes amount of tax that UN pays for American employees in its tax equalization program (M)

1998-06-29.title: His Critics Say President's Words Ring Hollow
1998-06-29.abstract: Clinton Administration officials praise President for his performance in Beijing, where he spoke out on human rights in televised news conference with Chinese Pres Jian Zemin, while his critics say he did only minimum that was required and that his words were largely empty (M)

1998-06-29.title: Alioune Blondin Beye, 59, Envoy for U.N.
1998-06-29.abstract: Alioune Blondin Beye, United Nations envoy on diplomatic mission aimed at averting renewed bloodshed in Angola, is apparently killed in crash of light plane near Abidjan, capital of Ivory Coast; he was 59 (N)

1998-06-29.title: Clinton's Call to Students: A 'Secure and Open' China
1998-06-29.abstract: Excerpts from Pres Clinton's remarks to students and faculty members at Beijing University (M)

1998-06-29.title: With Kosovo's Rebels: A Growing Confidence in Battle
1998-06-29.abstract: Rebel army in Kosovo Province, Serbia, has emerged as most powerful ethnic Albanian movement in region; decision by US envoy Robert Gelbard to meet with its representatives in Switzerland implicitly recognizes force as province's new power broker, and suggests that influence of Albanian leader Ibrahim Rugova, committed pacifist who described it as creation of Serb security forces, is waning; map (M)

1998-06-29.title: Rubin Could Find China The Easiest Part of His Trip
1998-06-29.abstract: Interview with US Treas Sec Robert Rubin, whose visit to China may be easiest part of trip to Far East; has been subject to fresh volley of criticism from Malaysian Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad, who accuses US Government of plotting with Wall Street to take over Asian economies (M)

1998-06-30.title: Bombay Journal; How India Offers the Feeding Without the Frenzy
1998-06-30.abstract: Intricate enterprise of Indian tiffin-wallah, which prepares and delivers home-cooked lunches to workers throughout major cities such as Bombay, has changed little in 130 years, despite services of fast-food outlets and American jet-borne courier companies; photo (M)

1998-06-30.title: Rubin Defends I.M.F. Policies and Continues His Asian Tour
1998-06-30.abstract: US Treas Sec Robert E Rubin, after whirlwind visit to Malaysia where he defended International Monetary Fund's harsh prescriptions for healing Asia's economies, arrives in Thailand; says he had good meeting with Malaysian Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad, who has been vituperative critic of West (M)

1998-06-30.title: SERB FORCES OPEN A MAJOR ASSAULT ON KOSOVO REBELS
1998-06-30.abstract: Special Serbian police units and troops, backed by artillery, tanks, and other heavy armor, launch major assault against separatist rebels in Kosovo Province; fighting reported near Belacevac and Lapushnik; Western diplomats say attacks end latest diplomatic effort to negotiate cease-fire and could lead to spread of fighting to other areas, including provincial capital of Pristina; new offensive follows efforts by US to broker cease-fire, but neither Belgrade nor rebel army, which controls 40 percent of province, are willing to make concessions; maps (M)

1998-06-30.title: Jiang Held His Cards Till Final Moment
1998-06-30.abstract: American officials say live television access to Chinese audience during Pres Clinton's meeting with Pres Jiang Zemin in Beijing represented breakthrough, but are unsure as to why Jiang allowed wide disemmination of Clinton's views; two-day meeting was fruit of risky decision in November 1996 to conduct summit meetings in Washington and Beijing without conditions, and thus no guarantee of success (M)

1998-06-30.title: Israeli President Urges Netanyahu to Call Early Elections
1998-06-30.abstract: Israeli Pres Ezer Weizman, in stunning and unusual slap at Netanyahu Government over its indecisive peace politics, publicly urges Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu to call early elections; Netanyahu rebuffs demand; says he intends to serve out four-year term, which expires in 2000; Weizman's move lends credibility and strength to idea that if Netanyahu is unable to make progress in long-stalled talks with his two-year-old coalition, he should take it to voters; observers say comments are deliberate attempt to make public his dismay and sense that he was deceived by Netanyahu (M)
